This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user drove a full-day session refactoring and stress-testing a GitHub-agentic-workflows (gh-aw) "agent factory" in the `measure-ai-proficiency` repo. Major explicit requests, in order:
   - Close redundant PR #133; discuss the sub-issue-layer merge-conflict problem.
   - "make the PR from here" for #136 (remove sub-issue layer) — then "NO not the plan make the final PR from here" — escalate the plan PR into a full implementation PR (#138).
   - Run a smoke test issue #140 through the refactored factory.
   - Produce a comprehensive analysis of the factory flow + GitHub Projects assessment; save as a living doc in `docs/`.
   - Analyze the #140 smoke test; fix findings including silent skipping of review workflows on Copilot PRs.
   - Use option (B) — gh-aw `bots:` frontmatter — to loosen the team-membership gate on review workflows; reanimate multi-implementer routing now that Partner Agents (Claude, Codex) are enabled.
   - Patch AGENT_FACTORY.md approval-gate docs.
   - File needs-spec issues #149 (reviewer auto-labels `needs-rebase`) and #152 (loosen protected_path_prefixes + reviewer self-tamper guard).
   - Convert fallback issue #153 into proper plan PR (#155).
   - Fix the `GH_AW_CI_TRIGGER_TOKEN` secret that doesn't exist (PR #156) after user screenshot revealed actual secrets list.
   - "do copilot only for now" + "do the cleanup" (PR #158): revert to Copilot-only routing and fix stale secret docs.
   - Swap #152 to `impl:copilot` after silent-drop.
   - Keep an eye on #157, #159 as they flow through the factory; eventually run a fresh smoke test after all fixes land.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - gh-aw (GitHub Agentic Workflows): markdown + YAML frontmatter workflows that compile to `.lock.yml` files.
   - Safe-outputs model: `assign-to-agent`, `assign-to-u
